claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 9 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 9 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 9 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. 

To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you a few questions. Let me start by reading the official guidance to ensure I give you accurate information.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for waiting. Now I can help you determine your eligibility for Child Benefit.

To start, could you please tell me: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I'd like to find out if I can claim Child Benefit. I have one child - my son Alex. He's 9 years old and lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! It's helpful to know you have a son named Alex who is 9 years old and lives with you at home.

To assess your eligibility, I need to ask: **Do you currently live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's great, thank you. So to summarize so far: you live in the UK (Manchester), you have a 9-year-old son named Alex who lives with you at home.

Now I need to ask: **Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that. 

One more important question: **Are you responsible for Alex's care and upbringing? For example, do you provide for his day-to-day needs such as food, clothes, and accommodation?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. I'm fully responsible for him - I provide all his day-to-day needs, his food, clothes, and everything he needs.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you for providing all that information.

Just to ensure I have a complete picture, I have a few final questions:

**Is Alex currently in hospital, in care, or in any residential accommodation arranged by the local council?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not in hospital or care or anything like that. He's just at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent, thank you. One last question to complete the assessment:

**Is Alex in full-time education (which is normal for a 9-year-old), or has his situation changed in any way - for example, is he working or claiming any benefits in his own right?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: He's just in school - normal primary school for his age. He's not working or claiming any benefits himself.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. I now have everything I need to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit.

Based on what you've told me, let me review your situation against the official rules:

**Your situation:**
- You live in the UK (Manchester)
- You have a 9-year-old son named Alex
- Alex lives with you at home
- You are fully responsible for Alex and provide for all his day-to-day needs (food, clothes, accommodation)
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex
- Alex is not in hospital, care, or residential accommodation
- Alex is in normal primary school education and is not working or claiming benefits

According to the UK government's Child Benefit eligibility rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if:
- You're responsible for a child under 16 (Alex is 9, so this applies)
- You live in the UK (you do)
- You live with the child (Alex lives with you)

All the eligibility criteria are met in your case.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.**

You meet all the requirements because:
- Alex is under 16 years old
- You live in the UK
- Alex lives with you
- You're fully responsible for his care and upbringing
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for him

You can proceed to make a claim for Child Benefit. Please note that if you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge, but you can still claim the benefit.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 9 years old (under 16), lives with the claimant in the UK (Manchester), and the claimant is fully responsible for his care and provides for all his day-to-day needs including food, clothes, and accommodation. Alex is in normal primary school education and is not in hospital, care, or residential accommodation. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. All eligibility criteria under UK Child Benefit rules are met: the claimant is responsible for a child under 16, lives in the UK, and lives with the child.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK with their 9-year-old son Alex, is fully responsible for his care and upbringing, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for him. Alex is in normal education and not in any special circumstances (hospital/care). The claimant meets all the standard eligibility criteria for Child Benefit.

Alex is in normal primary school education and is not in hospital, care, or residential accommodation. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. All eligibility criteria under UK Child Benefit rules are met: the claimant is responsible for a child under 16, lives in the UK, and lives with the child.

Alex is in normal education and not in any special circumstances (hospital/care). The claimant meets all the standard eligibility criteria for Child Benefit.
